name: Dean — Plex Server Cloudflare Tunnel Fix
topic: New Ubuntu server at 192.168.1.225 running Plex behind Cloudflare Tunnel not connecting via plex.deanhauser.dev

turns.content: I have set up a new ubuntu server running on 192.168.1.225 I set up a plex server with cloudflare tunnel at plex.deanhauser.dev but when I go on the website, the connection does nothing

This is a common issue with Plex behind Cloudflare Tunnels. A few things to check:

1. **Tunnel config — websockets and correct origin**: Make sure your tunnel points to http://localhost:32400 (or http://192.168.1.225:32400 if cloudflared runs elsewhere). Enable WebSockets under Additional application settings > Connection.

2. **Plex custom server access URLs**: In Plex Settings > Network > Custom server access URLs, add https://plex.deanhauser.dev. Set Secure connections to Preferred (not Required).

3. **Cloudflare SSL/TLS**: Set SSL/TLS mode to Full (not Full Strict). Make sure the DNS record for plex is proxied.

4. **Test the tunnel**: Run curl -I http://localhost:32400/web on the server and check sudo systemctl status cloudflared.

5. **Cloudflare TOS caveat**: Streaming large video through Cloudflare proxy is a gray area under Section 2.8.

Can you confirm how you set up the tunnel (dashboard vs CLI config) and what exactly happens when you hit plex.deanhauser.dev — blank page, timeout, error?
changing from localhost to the actual IP worked, thanks!
Glad that fixed it! The tunnel was likely not resolving localhost the way you'd expect. Enjoy the Plex server!
